title: [FIRRTL] width inference (reg) false positive
body:
The following FIRRTL program

```python
circuit top_mod:
  module top_mod:
    input clk: Clock
    input d: UInt
    output q: UInt
    reg _q: UInt, clk
    _q <= d
    q <= _q
```

Compiled with `firtool` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(
  input clk);

endmodule
```

Compiled with `firrtl-1.5-SNAPSHOT` produces this Error:

```
Exception in thread "main" firrtl.passes.PassExceptions: 
firrtl.passes.CheckWidths$UninferredWidth:  : Uninferred width for target below.serialize}. (Did you forget to assign to it?)
    circuit top_mod:
    └── module top_mod:
        └── d
firrtl.passes.CheckWidths$UninferredWidth:  : Uninferred width for target below.serialize}. (Did you forget to assign to it?)
    circuit top_mod:
    └── module top_mod:
        └── q
firrtl.passes.CheckWidths$UninferredWidth:  : Uninferred width for target below.serialize}. (Did you forget to assign to it?)
    circuit top_mod:
    └── module top_mod:
        └── _q
firrtl.passes.PassException: 3 errors detected!
```
